rationale: Design fidelity is high: the four-layer ratchet (R1), the 'config selects authority, never evidence' invariant (R7/R8, rigorously enforced via the config-free leaf + go-list-deps check), the stays-human floor (R5 panel_skip-key refusal + on_reject=halt), and the threshold-0 loophole closed both config- and record-side all faithfully operationalize research §3.2/§3.4. panel/models/loop/runner defaults match today's 3+3 / n-1 / opus-review-tier practice. The 109(substrate)/110(verbs)/111(runner) boundary is clean and non-overlapping. One genuine gap: research §3.2 L3's governance-profile YAML lists `handoff_log: AUTOPILOT-LOG.md`, and §3.4 names the morning handoff as one of four 'deliberately stays human' pillars. The spec operationalizes the other three but silently omits the handoff log from the `loop:` block — neither declared nor explicitly deferred, unlike every other deferral (models:/loop:/runner: enforcement) which the spec documents scrupulously. The spec's own 'declare-now-so-downstream-consumes' principle (applied to models:/runner:) argues for carrying it. This is the sole blocker.
concrete_changes_required: Requirement 4 (`loop:` governance skeleton): address the research §3.2 L3 `handoff_log: AUTOPILOT-LOG.md` key — the only key from the research's governance-profile YAML absent from the spec's loop block. Either (a) declare it as a config key (e.g. `loop.handoff_log`, default `AUTOPILOT-LOG.md`) so the substrate carries the morning-handoff artifact path the same way it carries every other not-yet-enforced governance knob and the ZeroConfig acceptance test asserts its default; or (b) add an explicit one-line deferral in Requirement 4 / Out-of-Scope stating the handoff log is intentionally not a config key at this layer and why — matching how the spec already documents the models:/loop:/runner: enforcement deferrals. As written it is neither declared nor deferred, while §3.4 elevates the morning handoff (the anti-comprehension-debt instrument) to a first-class 'stays human' pillar the spec otherwise honors.
findings: FAITHFUL — Four-layer ratchet (R1): (L1) gates / (L2) config / (L3) instruct / (L4) skills with the one-directional down-ratchet and the precedent chain (spec 102 in-binary gate, ADR-0037 matrix, ADR-0034 collapse) correctly synthesizes research §2.1's 'three blurred surfaces' plus the config 'middle rung' from §3.2. Adding config as the fourth (middle) layer is a faithful codification, not an invention.

FAITHFUL — 'Config selects authority, never evidence' invariant (research §3.2) preserved verbatim and rigorously: PanelGateDecision stays config-input-free (R8), internal/panel imports no internal/config (validation proof: go list -deps returns non-zero), ReviewerCountNote is a pure advisory that never flips Allow/Block, config is creation-time-default only. Strongest fidelity in the spec.

FAITHFUL — Stays-human floor (research §3.4) operationalized and appropriately strengthened: panel_skip removed from gate_authority AND its presence made a load error (stronger than research's literal `panel_skip: human` YAML, but consistent with §3.4 intent and the ADR-0037 §7 env-only rule); on_reject!=halt refused; threshold-0/negative rejected config-side and floored to N-1 record-side (grill-added defense-in-depth). The refuse-config vs safe-fallback-record asymmetry is coherently justified (human-authored config fails loud; the pure gate function must always return a decision, degrading strictly).

FAITHFUL — Defaults match today's practice: panel 3-claude+3-codex (=6), approve_threshold 'n-1', claude_sub_on_quota=true; loop halt 3/2/2 + on_reject=halt; budget 0=unlimited; context.controller_handoff=per-spec (§3.3); runner=claude-code-skills. loop.halt values match research §3.2 exactly. Moving panel_quorum out of loop: into a top-level panel: block is an improvement (panel mix matters for interactive panels too), not a drop — the keys survive.

FAITHFUL — 109/110/111 boundary matches the agreed split (substrate / verbs+writer / runner). Out-of-Scope explicitly defers the panel create/verify/tally verbs + panel.json writer to 110 and the workflow runner + non-default adapter dispatch to 111; runner: is declared in 109 and first consumed in 111; the R6 resolvers land in 109 for the 110 writer. No overlap.

FAITHFUL — models: free-form strings justified as runner-specific (R3/R10 tie to the portability principle); the five phases (implement/review/authoring/grill/final_review) map to real mindspec phases/skills, not invented labels. Enforcement honestly deferred (no in-binary agent spawner — stated).
GAP (the change above) — research §3.2 L3 `handoff_log` silently dropped from loop:; §3.4 morning-handoff pillar left with no config home while the other three pillars are operationalized.

MINOR (non-blocking) — research §4's 'all-one-family panel = degraded quorum that lowers the delegable gate set' is only half-captured: panel.substitution.claude_sub_on_quota declares the enable-substitution knob, but the degraded-quorum consequence is a runtime policy reasonably deferred with loop enforcement. No change required; noting for the 110/111 authors.
